acceptance_targets: [chapter_05_craft_001] PASS if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337 let a reader infer why 'Wakeblue' and 'Bridelight' spread and why Damaris has the story from on-page reactions alone, with no more than one explicit narrator-explanation sentence per passage; FAIL if the narration still repeatedly tells the reader why the joke or correction is working.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337 let a reader infer why 'Wakeblue' and 'Bridelight' spread and why Damaris has the story from on-page reactions alone, with no more than one explicit narrator-explanation sentence per passage; FAIL if the narration still repeatedly tells the reader why the joke or correction is working.
findings.problem: This is a comedy-driven humiliation chapter, but the narration repeatedly explains why the crowd's corrections and jokes land instead of trusting action, call-and-response, and fallout to do that work. Those interpretive glosses slightly deflate the comic pressure and slow the humiliation engine at moments that should stay sharper and meaner.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337 so the social logic arrives through observable behavior: who repeats the name, what surface changes, who laughs, who goes quiet, and who seizes the story. Keep the crowd-specific causality, but remove or recast enough narrator explanation that the scene lands as farce in motion rather than farce plus commentary.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
